text.title: Feature Flag Rollout Decision Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Feature Flag Rollout Decision must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every application produces a named artefact stored in a known location with a known schema. No artefact, no application.
Inputs to the method are explicitly listed with their source. Methods that 'use intuition' are rejected as un-auditable.

rationale.source: Decision-traceability principle from audit / compliance literature

rule.rationale: Decision-traceability principle from audit / compliance literature.

rule.statement: The method's output has a named consumer and a documented decision it feeds. Outputs nobody uses are removed.

rationale.source: Anti-orphan-artefact practice from kanban / lean ops

rule.rationale: Anti-orphan-artefact practice from kanban / lean ops.

rule.statement: The output names a single accountable owner (handle, email, or org role). No 'team' or 'we' as owner.
rule.rationale: RACI / single-throat-to-choke. Plural ownership = no ownership; future readers need a named human to chase.

rule.statement: Output carries semver version and last_reviewed date. Records older than 90 days are flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Methodology outputs decay; treating them as immutable is a defect. Versioning + review-by-date forces refresh cadence.

rule.statement: If any Skip-If clause from AGENTS.md fires OR any precondition fails, STOP applying this methodology and route to the correct one. Producing an artefact when the methodology does not apply is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mis-application is the most common methodology-routing failure observed in audits. An explicit STOP rule prevents the agent from synthesizing outputs for the wrong instrument.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Validate an AI feature before it consumes a quarter Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
